mnemonics.title: PETERS ON
mnemonics.content: P - Process (exactly 2) E - Entry section sets FLAG then TURN E - Exit section resets FLAG R - Respect: Turn gives way to other S - Starve-free with bounded waiting O - Only for 2 processes N - No hardware needed

acronyms.expansion: Boolean array indicating which process wants to enter critical section
acronyms.usage: flag[0] = true means Process 0 wants to enter

acronyms.expansion: Integer variable indicating whose turn it is to enter critical section
acronyms.usage: turn = 1 means it's Process 1's turn to enter if both want in
The portion of code where shared resources are accessed

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a bathroom with two people (P0 and P1). The flag is like a sign saying 'I want to use it'. The turn is like a ticket number. Only the person with the sign AND whose ticket is called can enter.
Think of it as: 'I want to go in (flag=true), but you can go first (turn=you). I'll wait as long as you want to go AND it's your turn.' This ensures only one enters.
Imagine a queue at a ticket counter. After I take a number (set flag), at most one person (the other process) can be served before me. That's bounded waiting - guaranteed!

analogies.analogy: Like a single-occupancy bathroom. Only one person can use it at a time. The flag is like putting a 'ied' sign onOccup the door.
Like deciding who goes first in a game when both players want to start. The turn variable is like flipping a coin - it decides who gets priority when there's a conflict.

analogies.analody: If no one is in the bathroom and someone wants to use it, they should be able to go in without unnecessary delay. It's like not making someone wait when the bathroom is clearly empty.
If you're waiting for the bathroom and someone else goes in, there's a limit to how many times others can go in before you get your turn. It's like a fair queue system.
